title: Partial-Year Depreciation
event: Hollis Co. buys a machine on April 1 for $96,000 with a $6,000 salvage value and a 5-year life, using straight-line depreciation. Compute depreciation for the year of acquisition.

computationPaths.narration: Assets earn depreciation only for the months they're in service — April 1 to December 31 is nine months.

memorize.body: Partial-year = full-year depreciation × months in service ÷ 12.
For accelerated methods the proration applies to each RATE-YEAR: an April 1 DDB asset's second calendar year mixes 3 months of year-1 rate with 9 months of year-2 rate.

questions.prompt: Machine in service April 1; full-year SL depreciation $18,000. First calendar year's depreciation = ?

distractors.feedback: That's a full year. The machine served only April–December: 18,000 × 9/12 = 13,500.
That's 8 months. April 1 through December 31 is NINE months: 13,500.
